Assembly: The assembly mLutLut1.2 is based on 63x PacBio data, 58x 10X Genomics Chromium data, BioNano data and 17x Hi-C data generated at the Baylor College of Medicine. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig identification with Purge Haplotigs, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Hi-C based scaffolding and improvement by the Aiden lab as part of a collaboration with DNA Zoo using 3D-DNA and Juicebox, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly mMarMar1.1 is based on 35x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes X and Y were assigned by read coverage statistics and synteny to GCA_029410595.1.

Assembly: The assembly mCanLor1.2 is based on 28x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Dovetail Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny to the GCA_014441545.1 assembly of Canis lupus familiaris.

Assembly: The assembly ilEpiTris1.1 is based on 84x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z assigned by alignment to Xanthorhoe spadicearia (GCA_947086425.1).

Assembly: The assembly daSyrVulg1.hap1.1 is based on 84x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The order and orientation of contigs is uncertain in the following regions: chromosome 5, 17.5 Mb to 20.1 Mb; chromosome 6, 24.7 Mb to 28.9 Mb; chromosome 8, 17.8 Mb to 22.5 Mb. Heterozygous inversions were observed in the following regions: chromosome 2, 32.1 Mb to 36 Mb; chromosome 2, 48.1 Mb to 50.9 Mb; chromosome 4, 24.4 Mb to 25.8 Mb; chromosome 8, 12.5 Mb to 16.3 Mb; chromosome 8, 38.9 Mb to 39.6 Mb; chromosome 11, nested inversion with breakpoints at 18.6 Mb, 19.1 Mb and 20.3 Mb; chromosome 11, 22.6 Mb to 26.8 Mb; chromosome 17, 31.6 Mb to end; chromosome 21, 11 Mb to 12.4 Mb.
